children.name: Anthreptes
children.description: Anthreptes — a genus of sunbirds in the family Nectariniidae.

children.name: Anthreptes anchietae
children.description: Anchieta's sunbird is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is found in Angola, the DRC, Malawi, Mozambique, Tanzania, and Zambia, and is named after José Alberto de Oliveira Anchieta.

children.name: Anthreptes aurantius
children.description: The violet-tailed sunbird is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is found near water in forest, scrub and mangrove in the Central African countries of Angola, Cameroon, Central African Republic, Republic of the Congo, Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Gabon. It is part of the violet-backed sunbird superspecies.

children.name: Anthreptes gabonicus
children.description: The mangrove sunbird or mouse-brown sunbird, is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. Its range covers areas near the Gulf of Guinea, from Senegal to northwestern Angola.

children.name: Anthreptes griseigularis
children.description: The grey-throated sunbird is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is found in a wide range of semi-open wooded habitats in the northern and eastern part of the Philippines. It is often considered a subspecies of the brown-throated sunbird, but the two differ consistently in measurements and plumage, and there is no evidence of intergradation between them.

children.name: Anthreptes longuemarei
children.description: The western violet-backed sunbird or Longuemare's sunbird is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is the most widely ranging species in the violet-backed sunbird superspecies, ranging throughout a large part of tropical mainland sub-Saharan Africa not inhabited by other members of the superspecies. It is mainly found in regions with mesic woodland.

children.name: Anthreptes malacensis
children.description: The brown-throated sunbird, also known as the plain-throated sunbird, is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is found in a wide range of semi-open habitats in south-east Asia, ranging from Myanmar to the Lesser Sundas and west Philippines. The grey-throated sunbird found in the remaining part of the Philippines is often considered a subspecies of the brown-throated sunbird, but the two differ consistently in measurements and plumage, and there is no evidence of intergradation between them.

children.name: Anthreptes neglectus
children.description: The Uluguru violet-backed sunbird is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. The bird is small with a short bill. The male is an iridescent upper body while the female has a brown upperbody.

children.name: Anthreptes orientalis
children.description: The eastern violet-backed sunbird, also known as the Kenya violet-backed sunbird, is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is found in arid savanna of East Africa, ranging from Djibouti in north to Tanzania in south. It is part of the violet-backed sunbird superspecies.

children.name: Anthreptes rectirostris
children.description: The yellow-chinned sunbird is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is found in West Africa, namely Sierra Leone to Ghana. The grey-chinned sunbird was formerly grouped with this species.

children.name: Anthreptes reichenowi
children.description: The plain-backed sunbird, also known as blue-throated sunbird is a sunbird. The sunbirds are a group of very small Old World passerine birds which feed largely on nectar, although they will also take insects, especially when feeding young. Flight is fast and direct on their short wings.

children.name: Anthreptes rhodolaemus
children.description: The red-throated sunbird is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is found in Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, Myanmar, the Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.

children.name: Anthreptes rubritorques
children.description: The banded green sunbird is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is endemic to Tanzania.

children.name: Anthreptes seimundi
children.description: The little green sunbird, also called Seimund's sunbird, is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is sometimes placed in the genus Nectarinia. It is widespread throughout the African tropical rainforest.

children.name: Anthreptes simplex
children.description: The plain sunbird is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is found in Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, Myanmar, Singapore, and Thailand. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical mangrove forests.

children.name: Arachnothera
children.description: Arachnothera — a genus of sunbirds in the family Nectariniidae.

children.name: Arachnothera affinis
children.description: The streaky-breasted spiderhunter is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is found in Java and Bali. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

children.name: Arachnothera chrysogenys
children.description: Arachnothera chrysogenys — a species in the genus Arachnothera, family Nectariniidae. Also known as sunbirds.

children.name: Arachnothera clarae
children.description: Arachnothera clarae — a species in the genus Arachnothera, family Nectariniidae. Also known as sunbirds.

children.name: Arachnothera crassirostris
children.description: The thick-billed spiderhunter is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is found in Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, Singapore, and Thailand. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

children.name: Arachnothera dilutior
children.description: Arachnothera dilutior — a species in the genus Arachnothera, family Nectariniidae. Also known as sunbirds.

children.name: Arachnothera everetti
children.description: The Bornean spiderhunter is a doubtful species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. The scientific name commemorates British colonial administrator and zoological collector Alfred Hart Everett.

children.name: Arachnothera flammifera
children.description: Arachnothera flammifera — a species in the genus Arachnothera, family Nectariniidae. Also known as sunbirds.

children.name: Arachnothera flavigaster
children.description: The spectacled spiderhunter is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is found in Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, and Vietnam. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.

children.name: Arachnothera hypogrammicum
children.description: Arachnothera hypogrammicum — a species in the genus Arachnothera, family Nectariniidae. Also known as sunbirds.

children.name: Arachnothera juliae
children.description: Arachnothera juliae — a species in the genus Arachnothera, family Nectariniidae. Also known as sunbirds.

children.name: Arachnothera longirostra
children.description: The little spiderhunter is a species of long-billed nectar-feeding bird in the family Nectariniidae found in the moist forests of South and Southeast Asia. Unlike typical sunbirds, males and females are very similar in plumage. They are usually seen in ones or twos and frequently make a tzeck call.

children.name: Cinnyris chloropygius
children.description: The olive-bellied sunbird is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is widely spread across the African tropical rainforest primarily throughout West and Central Africa, but is also found in Southern and East Africa.

children.name: Cinnyris ludovicensis
children.description: Ludwig's double-collared sunbird or the montane double-collared sunbird, is a small passerine bird which breeds in forested mountains above 1,800 m (5,900 ft) in western Angola as well as the Nyika Plateau of northern Malawi and northeastern Zambia. This bird is sometimes considered to be con-specific with the greater double-collared sunbird. It is sometimes placed in the genus Nectarinia.
